statement: A bakery made 247 cupcakes in the morning and 385 cupcakes in the afternoon. How many cupcakes did they make in total?
Maria has 56 stickers and her friend gives her 38 more. How many stickers does Maria have now?
A toy store had 523 toys. They sold 287 toys during the sale. How many toys are left?
hints: Start from the ones place: 3 - 7 requires borrowing

statement: A farmer plants 12 rows of apple trees with 8 trees in each row. How many apple trees are there in total?
hints: You can break it into (10 x 8) + (2 x 8)

statement: There are 7 bags with 6 oranges in each bag. How many oranges are there altogether?
hints: Or think of it as 7 x 6

statement: 48 students need to be divided equally into 6 teams. How many students will be on each team?
hints: We need to share 48 equally among 6 groups

statement: A school collected 1,248 books in January and 976 books in February for their library. How many books did they collect in total?
hints: Remember to carry when digits add up to more than 9

statement: Carlos has 3 shelves of books. Each shelf has 15 books. He gives away 12 books to his friend. How many books does Carlos have now?
hints: First find the total: 3 shelves x 15 books each
Then subtract the books he gave away

statement: A bus has 75 seats. If 48 people are sitting, how many empty seats are there?
hints: Subtract the people from the total seats
Start from the ones: 5 - 8 needs borrowing

statement: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. If you eat 3 slices, what fraction of the pizza did you eat?
hints: The total number of slices is the denominator
The slices you ate is the numerator
